payload.title: trust explain + WRITING-TRUST.md — proactive agent rule contract
payload.description:
The reactive loop (RT-70 structured diagnostics/fixes, RT-71 trust fix) tells an agent how to FIX a violation. The proactive half is missing: a single, authoritative, machine-GENERATED 'how to write Trust' an agent loads into context to write compliant code first-try. Prevention beats correction; every check→fix cycle is expensive.

Deliver:
1. Rule::instead() — the canonical one-line compliant idiom per rule, as DATA in the registry (today it's buried in visitor help-strings).
2. trust explain [<code>] [--format human|json] — on-demand rule reference (one rule or the full catalogue), agent-consumable via JSON.
3. docs/WRITING-TRUST.md — generated from the registry via cargo xtask gen-docs (always fresh), the loadable agent contract: every rule as ❌ don't / ✅ do / why.

This is the proactive complement to Tier 2's agent action stream — the most on-thesis 'for the agent' lever left.
